page: Microsoft Azure D96a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DAv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7452 processor, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The processor operates at a speed of 3.35 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 2458 GB of local storage. No GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core CPU performance in stress-ng is in the bottom 10%, multi-core performance is average. However, the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in memory-bound and multi-threaded operations. Cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth reaches 3919.03 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang also perform in the top 25%. Notably, the PassMark CPU encryption test achieves top-tier performance in the top 10% with a score of 96989.58, and Redis SET operations reach a strong 71.11 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D96a_v4 offers a high resource density of 96 vCPUs and substantial local storage, making it a viable option for workloads that benefit from parallel processing without requiring high single-thread performance. Tradeoffs include the lower single-core execution speed, which may bottleneck serial tasks. The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, database operations, Redis caching layers, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing with smaller models.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DAv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.35 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 384.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and 2458 GB of local storage, with no GPU hardware. Benchmarks show a clear tradeoff: single-core performance is relatively weak, but multi-core and memory-bound operations are highly capable. The instance delivers top-tier performance in CPU encryption and strong results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and Redis SET operations. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads that do not depend on high single-thread speeds. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and caching layers.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96a_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, built on Hyper-V virtualization.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core virtual machine with balanced memory and local storage, optimized for parallel processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB of system memory
Includes 2458 GB of local storage for data-intensive tasks
Top-tier performance in cryptographic encryption benchmarks
Strong multi-threaded decompression and software compilation speeds
Suited for databases, Redis caching, and parallelized workloads
